note: Squad resolution: touched impl files classify by convention as artifact-type skill/subagent, which the registry binds to the aced squad. ACED recuses on the SDD self-spec (its lens is agent-config rubric suites; SDD's own suites are boolean process-Gherkin) — established precedent, same path taken by CR github-191. Re-resolved this unit's chain to the SDD defaults (sdd-spec-judge / impl-producer-governance / sdd-impl-judge) and proceeded; not a fail-closed, no halt.

Edit class classified structurally per named Scenario via gherkin-cli diff --format json, not a raw line diff: impl-producer.feature added:5 modified:0 removed:0 addOnly:true; impl-judge.feature added:10 modified:0 removed:0 addOnly:true. Both additive -> self-clear, stay @frozen, no re-open, no Clearance floor. Mechanical pre-filter pnpm check:specs exit=0 before the cold judge was spawned.

Cold sdd-spec-judge R1: oracle PASS, builder PASS, architect FAIL, ALIGNED false. Real defect: the new absorption-read section header was inserted inside the pre-existing 'Structural read and rollup' section, stranding four unrelated scenarios (orthogonal structural read, structural blocker, and both rollup scenarios) under the absorption heading — a violation of suite-format's stage-grouping rule that would mislead a future reader or the impl-judge itself. Fixed by relocating the added block above the pre-existing header, leaving that section contiguous; the rollup correctly follows absorption since it aggregates every read. Verified a pure move: structural diff still addOnly:true, 0 modified, 0 removed, 27 pre-existing scenarios unchanged.

Cold sdd-spec-judge R1 CONTENT_GAP: the precondition/apparatus split had no operational discriminator, so a producer could relabel absorbed apparatus as 'precondition'. Judge deemed it non-blocking (escalate-don't-exempt closes it procedurally) but the gap was real. Adopted its proposed swap test into all three bars: substitute the Given's domain for an unrelated one; if the Then still holds, what was swapped is apparatus. Stated per-element, with 'an element that survives the swap and still appears in the artifact is absorbed — the producer's label decides nothing', so the discriminator is objective rather than self-declared. Also converted the judge's flagged weak abstract scenario in impl-producer.feature into the falsifiable swap-test assertion.

Cold sdd-spec-judge R2 (fresh context, carried nothing from R1): oracle FAIL, builder FAIL, architect PASS, ALIGNED false. It refuted a conductor decision, not a producer slip. The plan had settled 'no spec-producer.feature change — the producer self-aligns against the same governances the spec-judge grades with and already loads suite-format, so the bar reaches both actors.' Independently verified the refutation: spec-producer.feature's only self-check scenarios run the MECHANICAL feature-form check (hedge adverbs, form violations), while this same diff's suite-format text states probe independence has no deterministic form and is deliberately not in the mechanical pre-filter — so the one check the producer runs is, by the CR's own words, the wrong check for the duty. The reverse-direction (spec-absorbs-impl) duty reached the producer as prose with zero scenarios making compliance observable, asymmetric with the 5 scenarios impl-producer received for the mirror duty. The judge's framing stands: an unverified coverage claim is precisely the defect class this CR exists to catch, so the CR was held to its own standard. Fixed by covering (4 additive scenarios on the frozen spec-producer.feature + a Phase-2 authoring duty), not by narrowing the ratified scope. R1's two findings independently confirmed resolved by R2.

Cold sdd-impl-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS: all 20 added frozen scenarios re-derived independently as oracles (ADR-0016) and verified by static inspection against the delivered governance bodies — this artifact-type is doctrine prose with no runnable test and no verify-scenarios bridge, so full by-hand judging, no bridge fallback. Dogfood ran the NEW absorption read against the delivered text itself and verified the producer's independence claim by grep rather than trust: all 20 added Givens are abstract meta-preconditions about the rule itself carrying no concrete domain apparatus, and the one concrete illustration authored (a lookup key absent from its table, rendered as a purchase order then swapped to a sensor reading) shares no apparatus with any Given or pre-existing scenario. Judge noted this suite is materially lower-risk than #210, where genuine domain apparatus existed to lift — absorption was near-impossible here by construction, so a clean dogfood is weaker evidence than it looks. Polarity verified un-inverted; no lexical/n-gram probe smuggled in; no rationale prose; suite-format-governance/README.md contents refresh judged in-scope repair, not creep. pnpm verify 21/21.

Conductor hardening after the PASS (judge did not reject): the impl-judge's sole architect observation was that ABSORPTION_ESCALATIONS had no STATUS/QUESTIONS wiring, so a pending escalation could ride through as STATUS: complete. Escalated to the Council rather than deferred, because #220's own text indicts exactly this shape — the #210 sensor 'emitted a non-blocking observation, which became #211 and sat; there is no loop that routes it' — and shipping an unrouted escalation would reproduce the CR's complaint inside its own fix. Council ratified fixing before the gate. Wired a non-empty ABSORPTION_ESCALATIONS to STATUS: needs-input + QUESTIONS in sdd-impl-judge.md. Judge re-consulted on its own observation: closes it; scenario 10 now passes MORE faithfully (needs-input/QUESTIONS were already the doc's own declared vocabulary, so no imported strength, not an over-read); findings-gate-IMPLEMENTATION_PASS vs escalations-gate-STATUS confirmed orthogonal and coherent — a finding is a completed classification, an escalation an incomplete one, and forcing the latter to either pass or fail would be a silent pass or a coin-flip guilty. Rollup unchanged, pnpm verify 21/21.
